conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So, how has your week been so far? Any particular highlights or challenges that stood out to you?
Yeah, well, work's been a real challenge lately. I've been having trouble concentrating and making decisions, which is making me feel really anxious.
Can you tell me more about what's been going on at work that's got you feeling that way?
Honestly, it's just been a lot of stress trying to keep up with the patient load. I'm usually pretty good at my job, but lately, I've been making mistakes and feeling really overwhelmed.
I see. And how about outside of work? Have you had any opportunities to relax or do things you enjoy?
Not really. When I'm not working, I just feel exhausted. I used to love hiking on the weekends, but now it feels like too much effort.
That sounds tough. Do you find yourself having trouble sleeping at all?
Yeah, sometimes. I mean, I sleep fine most nights, but occasionally I'll wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there, feeling restless.
Can you tell me what's been going through your mind when that happens?
Honestly, it's usually just worries about work or things I need to do. I feel like I'm just not doing enough or meeting expectations.
That sounds really tough. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or energy levels lately?
Yeah, sometimes I don't really feel like eating dinner, and when I do eat, it's usually something quick and easy because I don't have the energy to cook.
It sounds like you've been struggling with some pretty big feelings. Are there any relationships in your life that are particularly important to you?
Yeah, my partner is really supportive. They're always telling me to take it easy and not push myself too hard.
That's great to have that kind of support system. How do you think your partner would describe your emotional state right now?
Hmm, I don't know... probably as stressed out? They've been noticing the changes in my mood and energy levels lately.
I see. Are there any hobbies or activities that you used to enjoy but haven't had time for recently?
Yeah, photography. I love taking pictures of nature, but since things got busy at work, I just haven't had the time or energy to go out and take photos like I used to.
That sounds really enjoyable. Are there any goals you have for this session or anything specific you'd like to work on?
Honestly, I'm not really sure... maybe just figuring out how to get my life back on track? Feeling more grounded and in control would be great.
I think we can definitely work towards that. Is there anything that's been on your mind lately that feels particularly stressful or overwhelming?
Yeah, it's the feeling of not being able to keep up with everything. It's like I'm just trying to hold water in my hands and it's slipping right out.
That sounds really frustrating. Have you noticed any physical sensations in your body when you're feeling that way?
Sometimes I get this knot in my stomach, like a tight band around my abdomen. It feels really uncomfortable.
Okay, that makes sense. What do you think might have triggered these feelings of overwhelm and stress lately?
I don't know... maybe just the constant pressure to perform at work? Or maybe it's something else entirely.
That's a good question. Are there any recent stressful events or changes in your life that might be contributing to this feeling?
Yeah, my boss has been breathing down my neck about meeting certain deadlines, and I've also got some personal stuff going on with my family.
Okay, it sounds like you're carrying a lot right now. Are there any coping strategies or support systems in place that have helped you deal with stress in the past?
Yeah, my partner is always telling me to take deep breaths and focus on the present moment. It helps a bit.
That's great. Do you think that might be something we could work on together during our sessions?
Yeah, maybe... I'd like to learn how to manage my stress better.
Okay, let's definitely work towards that. What are some small steps you can take this week to start taking care of yourself and reducing your stress levels?
Hmm, I guess just prioritizing self-care time, like taking a walk or reading before bed? And maybe trying to break down tasks into smaller, more manageable chunks.
That sounds like a great plan. What do you think is the most important thing for us to focus on during our sessions going forward?
Honestly, just finding ways to manage my stress and anxiety better. It feels like it's impacting every area of my life right now.
Okay, we'll definitely work towards that. Is there anything else you'd like to add or any concerns you have before we wrap up today?
No, I think that's it for now. Thanks for listening and being understanding.
